title: Red Hat Security Advisory: libssh security update
overview:
An update for libssh is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: libssh security update

An update for libssh is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for libssh is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
libssh-0.9.6-16.el8_10.src.rpm
libssh-0.9.6-16.el8_10.i686.rpm
libssh-0.9.6-16.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
libssh-config-0.9.6-16.el8_10.noarch.rpm
libssh-debuginfo-0.9.6-16.el8_10.i686.rpm
libssh-debuginfo-0.9.6-16.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
libssh-debugsource-0.9.6-16.el8_10.i686.rpm
libssh-debugsource-0.9.6-16.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
libssh-devel-0.9.6-16.el8_10.i686.rpm
libssh-devel-0.9.6-16.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
libssh-0.9.6-16.el8_10.s390x.rpm
libssh-debuginfo-0.9.6-16.el8_10.s390x.rpm
libssh-debugsource-0.9.6-16.el8_10.s390x.rpm
libssh-devel-0.9.6-16.el8_10.s390x.rpm
libssh-0.9.6-16.el8_10.ppc64le.rpm
libssh-debuginfo-0.9.6-16.el8_10.ppc64le.rpm
libssh-debugsource-0.9.6-16.el8_10.ppc64le.rpm
libssh-devel-0.9.6-16.el8_10.ppc64le.rpm
libssh-0.9.6-16.el8_10.aarch64.rpm
libssh-debuginfo-0.9.6-16.el8_10.aarch64.rpm
libssh-debugsource-0.9.6-16.el8_10.aarch64.rpm
libssh-devel-0.9.6-16.el8_10.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-5372: A flaw was found in libssh versions built with OpenSSL versions older than 3.0, specifically in the ssh_kdf() function responsible for key derivation. Due to inconsistent interpretation of return values where OpenSSL uses 0 to indicate failure and libssh uses 0 for success—the function may mistakenly return a success status even when key derivation fails. This results in uninitialized cryptographic key buffers being used in subsequent communication, potentially compromising SSH sessions' confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
